speaker: MENENIUS
text: See you yond coign o’ th’ Capitol, yond

speaker: MENENIUS
text: cornerstone?

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Why, what of that?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: If it be possible for you to displace it with

speaker: MENENIUS
text: your little finger, there is some hope the ladies of

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Rome, especially his mother, may prevail with

speaker: MENENIUS
text: him. But I say there is no hope in ’t. Our throats

speaker: MENENIUS
text: are sentenced and stay upon execution.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Is ’t possible that so short a time can alter the

speaker: SICINIUS
text: condition of a man?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: There is differency between a grub and a

speaker: MENENIUS
text: butterfly, yet your butterfly was a grub. This Martius

speaker: MENENIUS
text: is grown from man to dragon. He has wings;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: he’s more than a creeping thing.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: He loved his mother dearly.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: So did he me; and he no more remembers

speaker: MENENIUS
text: his mother now than an eight-year-old horse. The

speaker: MENENIUS
text: tartness of his face sours ripe grapes. When he

speaker: MENENIUS
text: walks, he moves like an engine, and the ground

speaker: MENENIUS
text: shrinks before his treading. He is able to pierce a

speaker: MENENIUS
text: corslet with his eye, talks like a knell, and his hum

speaker: MENENIUS
text: is a battery. He sits in his state as a thing made for

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Alexander. What he bids be done is finished with

speaker: MENENIUS
text: his bidding. He wants nothing of a god but eternity

speaker: MENENIUS
text: and a heaven to throne in.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Yes, mercy, if you report him truly.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I paint him in the character. Mark what

speaker: MENENIUS
text: mercy his mother shall bring from him. There is

speaker: MENENIUS
text: no more mercy in him than there is milk in a male

speaker: MENENIUS
text: tiger. That shall our poor city find, and all this is

speaker: MENENIUS
text: long of you.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: The gods be good unto us.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: No, in such a case the gods will not be good

speaker: MENENIUS
text: unto us. When we banished him, we respected not

speaker: MENENIUS
text: them; and he returning to break our necks, they

speaker: MENENIUS
text: respect not us.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Sir, if you’d save your life, fly to your house.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The plebeians have got your fellow tribune

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And hale him up and down, all swearing if

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Roman ladies bring not comfort home,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: They’ll give him death by inches.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: What’s the news?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Good news, good news! The ladies have prevailed.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: The Volscians are dislodged and Martius gone.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: A merrier day did never yet greet Rome,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: No, not th’ expulsion of the Tarquins.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Friend,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Art thou certain this is true? Is ’t most certain?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: As certain as I know the sun is fire.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Where have you lurked that you make doubt of it?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Ne’er through an arch so hurried the blown tide

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: As the recomforted through th’ gates. Why, hark you!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: The trumpets, sackbuts, psalteries, and fifes,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Tabors and cymbals, and the shouting Romans

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Make the sun dance. Hark you!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: This is good news.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I will go meet the ladies. This Volumnia

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Is worth of consuls, senators, patricians

speaker: MENENIUS
text: A city full; of tribunes such as you

speaker: MENENIUS
text: A sea and land full. You have prayed well today.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: This morning for ten thousand of your throats

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I’d not have given a doit. Hark, how they joy!

speaker: SICINIUS
text: First, the gods bless

speaker: SICINIUS
text: you for your tidings; next, accept my thankfulness.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Sir, we have all great cause to give great thanks.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: They are near the city?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Almost at point to enter.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: We’ll meet them, and help the joy.
